mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in the unemployment rate below 5.5 percent signals tightening labor market conditions that force firms to raise wages and prices, boosting nominal earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or zero correlation with 5-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and housing starts indicate synchronized real-sector expansion, which lifts aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day window.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy in more than 55 percent of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A persistent rise in the federal funds rate within a tightening cycle increases the cost of capital and compresses equity risk premiums, systematically depressing S&P 500 valuations and generating negative forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive or neutral in more than 50 percent of tightening episodes, or the historical correlation between rate hikes and equity declines breaks down over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly reflects expanding corporate sales and profit margins, which mechanically supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day trading window.
failure_condition: The rolling Spearman correlation between quarterly GDP growth surprises and 5-day forward equity returns remains below 0.05 across all available data periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month consumer price index increase exceeding 0.3 percent triggers market expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve intervention, which raises discount rates and compresses equity multiples, driving negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally or remain flat in more than 65 percent of instances following CPI spikes above the threshold, indicating decoupled monetary policy expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and retail sales demonstrates that wage gains are translating into actual consumer spending, which sustains corporate revenue trajectories and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction effect vanishes when controlling for interest rate changes, or the combined signal yields a negative information coefficient over any 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices signal improved capacity utilization and operational leverage for industrial and technology firms, which expands profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50 percent of rising INDPRO months, or the predictive power disappears when adjusted for concurrent FEDFUNDS movements.
